commands.review_summary:
The PR starts Gateway context-window cache warmup in the background during early startup, lets status wait briefly for an already-pending warmup, and adds focused tests for warmup and status behavior.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +71. Total +118 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main does not warm the context-window cache during Gateway startup and status reads use non-refreshing cache lookups. I did not run a live cold Gateway `/status` scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, which automation cannot provide for the contributor's external setup.

target.title: fix(status): warm gateway context cache in background
target.body:
Closes #92967.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where cold Gateway status paths could report fallback context-window values before startup-time model metadata discovery had a chance to populate the cache.

## Why This Change Was Made

Gateway startup now begins context-window cache warmup in the background from the startup config, and status briefly waits only for an already pending warmup before using the existing synchronous cache read. Startup readiness remains independent from catalog warmup completion.

## User Impact

Status output can show discovered context-window metadata earlier after Gateway startup without delaying Gateway readiness or crossing the lazy catalog import boundary.

## Evidence

Focused coverage passed for context warmup, status summary, status text, and Gateway early-startup behavior. The tests verify that Gateway early runtime returns while mocked warmup stays pending, status waits on pending warmup with a timeout, and startup config-based warmup populates discovered 1M Anthropic context metadata. The production build also completed from this branch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cold Gateway status paths can read discovered context-window metadata instead of falling back to 200K after startup warmup begins.
Real environment tested: macOS, focused source tests plus full build from this branch.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Exercised context warmup, status summary, status text, and Gateway early-startup behavior with the repo test wrappers, then ran the production build.
Evidence after fix: Gateway early runtime returned while a mocked cache warmup stayed pending; status waited on pending warmup with a timeout; startup cfg-based warmup populated discovered 1M Anthropic context metadata; production build completed.
Observed result after fix: Gateway startup is not blocked by context-window warmup, and status reads warmed context-window cache when startup warmup is available.
What was not tested: A live provider-connected Gateway cold-start recording.
target.author: turbotheturtle
